title: 18-Wheeler Crash in Arlington, Texas — Victim Guide (2026)

If you or someone you know has been involved in an 18-wheeler crash along Arlington's busy I-20, I-30, or SH-360 corridors, understanding your legal rights immediately is critical.

Truck accident cases are fundamentally different from standard auto claims. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) regulations govern hours-of-service, vehicle maintenance, and cargo loading — and violations can establish negligence. Electronic logging devices and black box data must be preserved quickly through formal spoliation hold letters, or that evidence may be lost forever.

reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in Texas — what evidence should I be trying to preserve and how long do I have?
reddit_body:
If you were recently hit by a semi-truck or 18-wheeler in Texas, the most important thing to understand is that the clock starts immediately. Trucking companies send rapid response teams to accident scenes within hours — their job is to protect the company's interests, not yours. Your priority should be sending a formal spoliation hold letter demanding preservation of the truck's black box (ECM), electronic logging device (ELD), dashcam footage, driver logs, and maintenance records before any of that data is overwritten or destroyed.

Under FMCSA regulations, commercial truck drivers are subject to strict hours-of-service rules, drug and alcohol testing requirements, and vehicle inspection standards. If any of those rules were violated, it can be powerful evidence of negligence. Texas also follows a modified comparative fault rule, meaning you can still recover damages even if you were partially at fault — as long as you're less than 51% responsible.

Truck accident cases are significantly more complex than standard car accident claims and usually involve multiple liable parties — the driver, the trucking company, the cargo loader, or even the truck manufacturer. If you're trying to find qualified legal help in the Arlington or DFW area, the folks at wreckmatch.com have a network of attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases and can match you at no cost.
